_instructions: Add titles here. Set 'done': true when reviewed. 'score' and 'notes' are optional pre-logs of your gut reaction — if set, the AI will write to that score. This is the ONLY queue file the daily review bot reads (data/queue.json) — do not create or edit a queue.json at the repo root, it is ignored by the script.

movies.title: Saw
movies.notes: Amazing. Great concept, great acting, low budget but it worked perfectly. One of the best horror debuts in years — the concept alone earns its place in the genre.

movies.title: Saw II
movies.notes: Became more gruesome and disgusting — loved it. A worthy follow-up that dials up the nastiness without losing the grip of the original.

movies.title: Saw III
movies.notes: Same old same old with some things here and there. Jigsaw dying of brain cancer on the table while Amanda runs the traps is the most interesting thread — the rest is starting to feel familiar.

movies.title: Saw IV
movies.notes: Not much to say. It's there, it does Saw things, but it doesn't leave much of an impression either way.

movies.title: Saw V
movies.notes: More of the same, unfortunately. The deaths are still there and I still like them, but everything around them is stale. The franchise is running on fumes.

movies.title: Saw VI
movies.notes: A bit better than the last few — the health insurance angle gives Jigsaw's philosophy an actual target for once. Nothing amazing but a slight step back up.

movies.title: Saw 3D: The Final Chapter
movies.notes: Didn't know what it was supposed to be and it didn't work for me. Marketed as the definitive end, shot in 3D, brought back Dr. Gordon — none of it added up to anything satisfying.

movies.title: Jigsaw
movies.notes: Soft reboot after seven years away — kinda okay but meh overall. The mystery of who's running the game is fine but it doesn't reignite anything.

movies.title: Spiral: From the Book of Saw
movies.notes: Read the reviews after years of disappointment, watched 30 minutes and said no to myself. Never finished it. From what I saw I wouldn't rate it higher than a 4 — it wants to be a crime thriller more than a Saw film and fails at both.

movies.title: X
movies.notes: Genuinely surprised by this one — really enjoyed it. Weird in the best way, and Ti West clearly knew exactly what vibe he was going for. Unique and well executed. One of the better recent slashers.

movies.title: Pearl
movies.notes: The prequel to X set in 1918 — kind of confusing in where it fits but for what they were going for it worked fine. An origin story that earns its place even if it's the stranger of the two films.

movies.title: The Descent Part 2
movies.notes: A continuation of The Descent that didn't really need to exist, but what they did with it was fine. Captures enough of the original's claustrophobic tension to justify itself.

movies.title: The Thing
movies.notes: The 1982 original with Kurt Russell is close to a perfect 10 and didn't need a prequel. For what they were going for this is a decent enough movie, but it can't escape the shadow of what came before it.

movies.title: V/H/S
movies.notes: Super terrifying in parts — the anthology format works well here and the horror segments are gory and effective. Not perfect since the quality varies between segments, but as a whole it delivers.

movies.title: V/H/S/2
movies.notes: Same format, different stories, same vibe — and that's the problem. Mediocre follow-up that doesn't bring anything new to what the first one already did.

movies.title: V/H/S: Viral
movies.notes: Deals with cult-related content among other things. Slightly more interesting than V/H/S/2 but still well within the diminishing returns territory of the series.

movies.title: V/H/S/94
movies.notes: The standout segment — a woman in a UFO transforming into some kind of creature — was genuinely terrifying and disgusting in the best way. One of the more effective entries in the series because of moments like that.

movies.title: V/H/S/99
movies.notes: Can't fully remember it, which says enough. Felt like a try-hard entry — interconnected segments but none of them landed with any real impact.

movies.title: V/H/S/85
movies.notes: Okay but nothing special. Being generous with the 6 — the franchise has settled into a comfortable mediocrity at this point.

movies.title: Insidious
movies.notes: Really interesting concept — the other world, astral projection, a different kind of horror that felt genuinely fresh. Cool and unsettling in the right ways.

movies.title: Insidious: Chapter 2
movies.notes: A quite decent continuation that picks up immediately where the first left off. Keeps the same energy and expands the mythology without losing what made the original work.

movies.title: Insidious: Chapter 3
movies.notes: More of the same — solid but the formula is becoming familiar. Still watchable and holds the standard of the first two well enough.

movies.title: Insidious: The Last Key
movies.notes: Really dumb. The franchise is running out of ideas and it shows — the plot goes places that don't hold up and the scares feel recycled.

movies.title: Insidious: The Red Door
movies.notes: Can't even remember it, which tells you everything. Bad enough to leave no impression whatsoever.

movies.title: The Conjuring
movies.notes: Amazing, terrifying, exactly what I wanted from a ghost movie after Paranormal Activity. Absolutely creepy and genuinely well crafted. One of the best haunted house films in years.

movies.title: Annabelle
movies.notes: Kind of worked. The redesigned doll is less terrifying than the real Annabelle — the original straw doll is somehow more unsettling than anything they put on screen — but the film itself holds up well enough.

movies.title: The Conjuring 2
movies.notes: Quite decent. Following the Warrens through another case worked, and the Enfield Poltergeist setting in England gave it something different. Based on a real case that was later partly exposed as faked for publicity — which is kind of sad but doesn't take away from the film.

movies.title: Annabelle: Creation
movies.notes: The prequel to Annabelle — orphans in a house with the doll. Had some nice moments but overall felt like nonsense. Doesn't hold together well enough to justify its existence.

movies.title: The Nun
movies.notes: Cheap jump scares, absolutely horrendous. The Nun as a character was already terrifying on her own — giving her an entire movie was a mistake. It dilutes everything that made her effective.

movies.title: The Curse of La Llorona
movies.notes: Same problem as The Nun — jump scares, clichés, didn't need its own movie. Horrible from start to finish. A character that belongs in the background handed a full film and given nothing interesting to do.

movies.title: Annabelle Comes Home
movies.notes: An unnecessary extension of the universe. Features the Warrens but doesn't do anything meaningful with them. The Conjuring universe stretching itself far too thin.

movies.title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
movies.notes: A bit better than the spinoffs — more of a court case thriller than a horror story, which is a strange direction. Based on a real murder case where the defendant pleaded demonic possession. The idea is interesting but the execution feels dumb. Still, it resonated more than the spinoffs.

movies.title: The Nun II
movies.notes: Even worse than the first Nun. Horrendous jump scares telegraphed from miles away, horrendous setting, horrendous acting — everything about this was baffling and bad. A new low for the Conjuring universe.

movies.title: Scream
movies.notes: Quite terrifying, really cool, really well set up. The twist at the end landed perfectly. One of the rare horror films that felt genuinely fresh when it came out.

movies.title: Scream 2
movies.notes: A solid continuation but you already know the formula by now and the film can't escape that. Still enjoyable — just can't replicate the surprise of the first.

movies.title: Scream 3
movies.notes: The formula is wearing thin. Watchable but nothing about it stands out.

movies.title: Scream 4
movies.notes: Can't fully remember it, which tells you something. Didn't really enjoy it — the self-awareness angle feels exhausted at this point.

movies.title: Scream
movies.notes: A soft reboot with Jenna Ortega that I kind of enjoyed as a guilty pleasure. Almost works. That said — the legacy characters coming back, Sidney included, don't actually add anything to the film. I'm not against legacy returns when they're done well, but here they feel like a checkbox rather than a reason to care. The new cast has to carry it regardless and mostly does.

movies.title: Child's Play
movies.notes: One of the best creepy doll horror films ever made. Super creepy, brilliantly executed — Charles Lee Ray coming to life in a small house setting with good acting all around. Loved every minute of it.

movies.title: Child's Play 2
movies.notes: A decent continuation with a bigger budget. The ending was quite brutal for Chucky and I loved it for that — but it doesn't match the original's tension.

movies.title: Child's Play 3
movies.notes: One of the last entries where Chucky still felt somewhat serious — Andy going to military school gave it a different setting that sort of worked. Not entirely, but okay.

movies.title: Bride of Chucky
movies.notes: This is where the franchise went amiss for me — too campy, too comedic. Tiffany changes the dynamic and Chucky loses some of his creepiness. Not terrible but a clear shift in the wrong direction.

movies.title: Seed of Chucky
movies.notes: Fully a comedy at this point rather than a horror film. Has some nice kills in it but the horror is essentially gone. The franchise lost the plot here.

movies.title: Curse of Chucky
movies.notes: A return to horror after the comedy detour — less campy, different setting, and it kind of works. Not a full recovery but a step in the right direction.

movies.title: Cult of Chucky
movies.notes: Sort of worked, sort of didn't. Continuing the more serious tone of Curse but not quite landing it either.

movies.title: Child's Play
movies.notes: An abomination of a remake. They should have named it something else entirely — maybe an adjacent killer doll concept rather than Chucky. The Chucky name only highlights how far this misses the mark. Could have worked as its own thing.

movies.title: Ringu
movies.notes: Decent — better than The Ring Two but not better than the American original, which might be controversial. People hyped it as terrifying and it was solid, just not the revelation others made it out to be. The American version landed harder for me personally.

movies.title: The Ring
movies.notes: Absolutely terrifying when I first saw it. Naomi Watts was great, the little girl was great, the whole thing was genuinely unsettling and felt unique. Great scares throughout — one of the stronger American horror remakes ever made.

movies.title: The Ring Two
movies.notes: Didn't need to exist but made it anyway off the back of the first one's success. The crawling out of the well sequence was genuinely terrifying — that one moment alone earns it some credit. But it doesn't have the oomph of the original and the rest doesn't hold up.

movies.title: Rings
movies.notes: Only watched snippets — couldn't bring myself to sit through the whole thing. Felt like a near-retelling of the first American film with none of what made it work. From what I saw, a 4 is generous.

movies.title: Chucky
movies.notes: Watched through season 1, 2, and part of 3 before they took it to the Oval Office and I was out. Has some solid kills in seasons 1 and 2 but the further it goes the more it loses the thread. Stopped caring before they jumped the shark completely.

movies.title: Halloween
movies.notes: One of the best slashers ever made. Felt genuinely realistic in the way it was filmed and executed — the craft is undeniable. A benchmark for the genre.

movies.title: Halloween II
movies.notes: Same energy as the first, just a continuation. Picks up immediately where the original left off and does enough to justify its existence.

movies.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
movies.notes: No Michael Myers whatsoever, which was genuinely devastating. An anthology approach that might work on its own terms but as a Halloween sequel it's a baffling choice.

movies.title: Halloween 4: The Return of Michael Myers
movies.notes: Different mask again — with every sequel he seems to get a new one and it never quite works. The film itself doesn't do much to distinguish itself either.

movies.title: Halloween 5: The Revenge of Michael Myers
movies.notes: More of the same. The franchise is treading water at this point.

movies.title: Halloween: The Curse of Michael Myers
movies.notes: A slight step up from the previous two entries — the cult angle adds something different even if it doesn't fully land.

movies.title: Halloween H20: 20 Years Later
movies.notes: Jamie Lee Curtis back as Laurie Strode after 20 years in hiding — and it actually works. A legitimate sequel that respects the original and gives Laurie a proper arc. One of the stronger entries in the franchise.

movies.title: Halloween: Resurrection
movies.notes: Busta Rhymes. An internet reality show set in the Myers house. Incredibly campy and doesn't fit the franchise at all. Barely remember it and what I do remember didn't work.

movies.title: Halloween
movies.notes: Rob Zombie's take actually worked for me. Loved the intro especially — the extended origin and young Michael section gave the character real weight. Not the original but a solid reimagining.

movies.title: Halloween II
movies.notes: Got a lot of undeserved hate. A continuation of what Zombie set up in the first and it works for what it's going for — not a high standard thing but a decent follow-through on his vision.

movies.title: Halloween
movies.notes: A direct sequel to the 1978 original that skips everything in between — and it mostly works. A clean soft reboot that brings Laurie and Michael back without the baggage of 40 years of sequels.

movies.title: Halloween Kills
movies.notes: Absolutely abysmal. Not necessarily worse than every other bad Halloween sequel but genuinely bad nonetheless.

movies.title: Halloween Ends
movies.notes: Abysmal. The way they kill Michael Myers here is flat-out disgusting — not in a good horror way, in a disrespectful-to-the-character way. A terrible end to a trilogy that started with genuine promise.

movies.title: Friday the 13th
movies.notes: One of the best slashers next to Halloween. Great kills, great villain, the whole cabin setting worked perfectly. Reminded me a bit of Evil Dead in the best way. Thoroughly enjoyed this one.

movies.title: Friday the 13th Part 2
movies.notes: Continues the story well — another very enjoyable sequel that keeps the energy of the original going.

movies.title: Friday the 13th Part III
movies.notes: Same old same old but still watchable. Holds up reasonably well even if it's not adding anything new.

movies.title: Friday the 13th: The Final Chapter
movies.notes: Called itself the final chapter but we all know how that went. Fine for what it is — the Jason kills and death moments are always unique in their own way but there's not much else here.

movies.title: Friday the 13th: A New Beginning
movies.notes: Worth noting this is the one where Jason doesn't actually appear — it's a copycat killer the whole time. The kills are fine but it's the odd one out of the series for a reason.

movies.title: Friday the 13th Part VI: Jason Lives
movies.notes: This is the one where Jason is actually resurrected by lightning — the real comeback entry. Don't remember much of it beyond that but it did what it needed to do.

movies.title: Friday the 13th Part VII: The New Blood
movies.notes: The franchise is running on fumes here. Not much to distinguish it from what came before.

movies.title: Friday the 13th Part VIII: Jason Takes Manhattan
movies.notes: A different setting for once which was cool, but they fumbled it — most of the film isn't even in Manhattan. The idea was better than the execution.

movies.title: Jason Goes to Hell: The Final Friday
movies.notes: Interesting idea — Jason as a possessing force jumping between bodies — but the execution let it down badly. Doesn't feel like a Friday the 13th film.

movies.title: Jason X
movies.notes: Maybe controversial but I actually liked the idea of Jason in space. I like sci-fi and the concept is so absurd it circles back around to fun. Not a good film but an entertaining one.

movies.title: Freddy vs. Jason
movies.notes: A lot of people hate this but it's campy and it delivers exactly what you came for — Freddy and Jason beating each other up. The kids are just collateral damage and that's fine. The ending even sets up a sequel we never got, which is a shame. It's stupid, it's camp, but it gave the fans what they wanted and that counts for something.

movies.title: Friday the 13th
movies.notes: Genuinely enjoyed the remake. Nothing groundbreaking but decent for what it was — a solid modern take that respects the source material without overthinking it.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street
movies.notes: Fantastic. Something completely new at the time — atmospheric, disturbing, and genuinely original. The bathtub scene became an iconic shot to me personally, maybe not mainstream pop culture iconic but it stuck with me in a way few horror images do.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 2: Freddy's Revenge
movies.notes: Kind of an extension of the first one — Freddy possessing the boy from the inside rather than invading dreams was a different angle but it didn't do much for me. Some nice deaths but that's about it.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 3: Dream Warriors
movies.notes: This one was a genuine surprise. Dream Warriors works really well — highly entertaining, great deaths, Nancy coming back as a counsellor was a smart move. This should've been the definitive sequel and in many ways it is.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 4: The Dream Master
movies.notes: Did not like it at all. Picks off the Dream Warriors survivors one by one and replaces them with a new lead — it loses everything that made the previous entry work.

movies.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street 5: The Dream Child
movies.notes: Didn't know what to make of this one. Freddy using an unborn child to enter the real world felt weird — the pregnancy angle is a dark premise but it doesn't go anywhere interesting with it.

movies.title: Freddy's Dead: The Final Nightmare
movies.notes: Kinda meta, which is why I liked it — there's a self-awareness to it that works here more than it has any right to. Different Freddy look as well, which landed. Played for laughs in parts and it actually worked. An entertaining send-off even if it's a strange one.

movies.title: Hellraiser
movies.notes: Disgusting deaths, genuinely unique, grotesque in the best way. Hell as a concept felt real and horrifying here. One of the most distinctive horror films ever made.

movies.title: Hellbound: Hellraiser II
movies.notes: Feels like a bigger budget from the looks of it. Interesting continuation — held my attention and built on the original well enough to be worth watching.

movies.title: Hellraiser III: Hell on Earth
movies.notes: Disgusting deaths but that's about it. The franchise starts going stale here — the kills are there but nothing else holds it up.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Bloodline
movies.notes: Gruesome deaths kept me engaged but the story didn't interest me at all. The kills are doing all the heavy lifting and it's not enough.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Inferno
movies.notes: Interesting premise — felt like early Saw to me, that same kind of detective-in-a-trap setup. The cop with hooks in his face was something else. A step above the previous two entries but not by a huge margin.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Hellseeker
movies.notes: Can't fully recall it, which tells you everything. Wasn't amused. Went in one eye and out the other.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Deader
movies.notes: So cheap I felt like I went to Hades myself during the film. Production value is non-existent.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Hellworld
movies.notes: Interesting premise on paper — completely flatlined in execution. Two Hellraiser films in the same year and neither one had any business existing.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Revelations
movies.notes: So cheap in every possible way — the acting, the production, all of it. One of the worst entries in any horror franchise I've seen. A new low.

movies.title: Hellraiser: Judgment
movies.notes: Not as forgettable as the entries before it — they clearly tried this time. But trying and succeeding are different things and this still falls flat.

movies.title: Hellraiser
movies.notes: First time a woman as the lead, which I didn't expect, and it landed somewhat. Absolutely gruesome — a genuine attempt to go back to the roots of the original with some nice practical effects. A great effort and return for the franchise even if it doesn't fully get there.

tv.title: The Haunting of Hill House
tv.notes: The jump scares genuinely got to me — the coffin scene where the camera pans around and the girl suddenly appears, the girl in the car, the ghost with the cane hitting the floor while floating. All hit hard. Great performances across the board. One of the best haunted house stories put to screen.

tv.title: The Haunting of Bly Manor
tv.notes: After Hill House it felt more of the same and slipped into cliché territory. Still had great scenes and strong performances that kept me engaged throughout, but it never matches what came before it.

tv.title: The Fall of the House of Usher
tv.notes: Slow start — the beginning was meh — but the story builds and gets better as it progresses. Never reaches Hill House in terms of execution but it finds its footing and delivers enough to be worth it.

games.title: Alien: Isolation
games.notes: Why not a perfect 10 you ask? At certain times especially on the hardest difficulty, I find certain things a bits bit more annoying, yeah you got less health makes sense but the Alien legit hears everything, even from a mile away and it can sprint so fast even when you don't hear it, apart from that when you get the flamthrower sometimes the Alien doesn't get bothered by it and still kill you, very frustrating moments, thats why I give it the grade I give it.
